text.title: claude-code-headless-default Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for claude-code-headless-default. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For any new agent that runs a coding, devops, or research task on a machine with a shell, the first implementation MUST be a `claude -p` subprocess invocation. A custom Agent SDK loop is allowed only after the CLI has been demonstrated to fail one of the four switch-triggers below.
rule.rationale: The CLI harness encodes battle-tested decisions (retry, compaction, tool gating) maintained by Anthropic. Building a custom SDK loop first re-pays that cost without measurable benefit.

rule.statement: Headless invocations MUST set `-p`/`--print`, an explicit `--allowedTools` list, `--max-turns`, and `--output-format stream-json --verbose` so the orchestrator can parse events line-by-line.
rule.rationale: Without `-p` the binary opens a TUI; without `--allowedTools` the agent stalls on permission prompts; without `--max-turns` a runaway loop can drain budget; without stream-json the orchestrator only sees the final blob.
